qualitative_summary: The v0.2.x rules (HTML-comment format, full-mode section) provided clear guidance in 14 of 15 prompts. Sequence, hierarchy, comparison, priority, branching, state-machine, and mapping prompts all triggered diagrams reliably — the rules name each structure type explicitly with syntax examples, leaving little ambiguity. The 'none' class (3 prompts: single fact, term definition, recommendation) correctly produced prose-only responses because the rules list 'single fact or direct answer' as an explicit no-diagram case. The one ambiguous case was status-small (4 tasks): the full-mode rule gates frame blocks at 'more than five lines', so a 4-task list correctly used a plain bullet list instead of a frame — this threshold was clear but required careful reading. One drafting error was caught during generation (two bracket-boxes on the same line in branch-01, L05 violation), which was fixed before final output. The v0.2.x format relies on HTML comment delimiters to separate intensity modes, which is readable but less machine-parseable than the v0.3.x XML contract; the rules themselves are sufficiently specific to guide correct diagram-or-not decisions in nearly all cases.
tokens_spent_estimate: ~6500 tokens (sum of response_chars across 15 prompts: 6853 chars, plus system overhead)
